purpose: Reusable URL-template knowledge base for an LLM or other string-to-URL search caller. Focused on language/vocabulary tools, scholarly/scientific search, and trusted official/general reference resources.

method_summary.selection_rule: Finalists were chosen for high utility, clear query construction, broad coverage of the requested domains, and either direct testing or strong documentation.

encoding_baseline.normalize_unicode: NFKC optional; safe for most user text before URL building.
encoding_baseline.default_path_segment_encoding: Percent-encode one path segment via quote(s, safe='') (space -> '%20', '/' -> '%2F').

operator_preservation_rule.instruction: When the endpoint supports field tags, quotes, Boolean operators, wildcards, ranges, or other query syntax, preserve those operators in the logical query string and percent-encode only when inserting into the final URL. Do not aggressively scrub punctuation inside the logical query.

file_extension_rule.instruction: For extension lookups, strip a leading dot if present, lowercase the extension, and encode as a path segment. Example: '.PDF' -> 'pdf'.

phrase_handling_rule.instruction: For ordinary phrase search on query-parameter endpoints, trim, collapse repeated whitespace to single spaces, then quote_plus the phrase. If the site supports exact-phrase quoting and exact phrase is intended, wrap the logical phrase in double quotes before URL encoding.

word_lookup_rule.instruction: For exact single-word dictionary/etymology lookups that use path segments, pass the original token as one path segment after trimming. Encode apostrophes, spaces, slashes, and question marks.

finalists.label: English dictionary / exact headword lookup

steps: do not strip apostrophes or punctuation

finalists.notes: Best for exact dictionary-style lookup.

finalists.label: Synonyms / antonyms / related words
finalists.notes: Use when synonym/antonym intent is explicit.

finalists.label: Learner-friendly English dictionary
finalists.notes: Good secondary dictionary, especially for learner-oriented definitions and pronunciation.

finalists.label: Etymology / word origin / word history
finalists.notes: Best for etymology among the finalists.

finalists.label: Exact term lookup with multilingual and morphological context
finalists.notes: Useful when morphology, alternate forms, or multilingual sense inventory matters.

finalists.label: General knowledge search with title redirect on exact match
finalists.notes: Wikipedia may redirect to a title page when it finds an exact title match; otherwise it yields search results.

finalists.label: Dictionary aggregator / reverse dictionary / pattern search / misspelling-adjacent discovery

steps: preserve pattern syntax if used (* ? # @ // minus/plus suffixes, or pattern:meaning)

finalists.notes: Very useful for unknown/misspelled/contextual word hunting. May present JavaScript/CAPTCHA friction to automated clients. Same endpoint also supports reverse-dictionary and pattern syntax.

finalists.label: Biomedical literature search

steps: preserve PubMed operators/field tags/quotes if present
otherwise collapse whitespace and quote_plus the query

finalists.notes: PubMed citation sensor can often recover from imperfect citation strings and some incorrect terms.

finalists.label: Clinical evidence-focused PubMed search
finalists.notes: Use when the intent is clinical evidence rather than general biomed indexing.

finalists.label: Consumer health topic retrieval from NIH/NLM
finalists.notes: Machine-readable XML; useful when a safer, patient-oriented medical topic source is preferable to journal literature.

finalists.label: Life sciences literature search
finalists.notes: Strong complement to PubMed; good global life-sciences coverage.

finalists.label: Open preprint search across many scientific and technical fields

steps: advanced usage may preserve arXiv-style boolean expressions

finalists.notes: Best open starting point for recent ML/AI/algorithms work.

finalists.label: Scholarly works search API

steps: preserve OpenAlex search syntax when intentionally used

finalists.notes: Machine-readable and broad; excellent for LLM ingestion.

finalists.label: Crossref bibliographic metadata search
finalists.notes: Good for DOI-oriented and bibliographic retrieval.

finalists.label: NASA aerospace and engineering literature search
finalists.notes: Very strong for aerospace engineering, NASA technical reports, and some space systems software/analysis topics.

finalists.label: Humanities/social sciences and some interdisciplinary journal/book search

steps: preserve quotes if exact phrase is wanted

finalists.notes: Useful but not as open-machine-friendly as arXiv/OpenAlex/Crossref.

finalists.label: Open-access journal/article discovery
finalists.notes: DOAJ quick search is prominently documented on the home page. Path-style article search URL is widely used, but less explicitly documented than some API endpoints.

finalists.label: Python documentation search
finalists.notes: OpenSearch template for Python docs explicitly publishes this URL pattern.

finalists.label: NumPy documentation search
finalists.notes: Search results are rendered via Sphinx search page.

finalists.label: SciPy documentation search
finalists.notes: Search results are rendered via Sphinx search page.

finalists.label: PyTorch documentation search
finalists.notes: PyTorch search page works, although it warns that JavaScript improves the experience.

finalists.label: Web platform / browser API documentation search
finalists.notes: MDN itself uses /en-US/search?q=... in examples and site URLs.

finalists.label: File extension lookup
finalists.notes: One of the most practical direct filetype resources.

finalists.label: US government open dataset search
finalists.notes: Good general U.S. government dataset discovery entry point.

finalists.label: General Library of Congress search endpoint
finalists.notes: Useful official reference endpoint; JSON is convenient for LLM parsing.

researched_candidates.site_name: USGS water services URL generation tool
researched_candidates.notes: Specialized URL builder for USGS water service endpoints.
Likely useful, but I kept Data.gov and LoC as the more clearly documented finalists.
Likely usable, but the web service is cleaner for machine consumption.
